page.content: In the assessment of the baseline characteristics of participants in this study, we focused on key metrics that provide insight into the differences between the Adjuvant Arthritis group and the Control group. The data table presents a comparative analysis of various components, with particular attention to the demographic and clinical characteristics of the two cohorts. Notably, the metrics of interest include the mean age and gender distribution, which are critical for understanding the baseline equivalence of the groups prior to intervention.

page.content: The sample size consisted of 18 individuals in the Adjuvant Arthritis group and 20 in the Control group, facilitating a robust comparison. The P values indicated in the table serve to highlight statistically significant differences between the two groups, thereby reinforcing the importance of these baseline characteristics in the overall study design. Understanding these initial conditions is essential for interpreting the subsequent outcomes and evaluating the efficacy of the interventions being studied. Thus, the baseline data not only contextualizes the findings but also ensures that any conclusions drawn are grounded in a thorough examination of participant characteristics at the outset of the study.
